ab: no kalialikey ko pa am mivahay namen do Jivalino, mivatvaket ko rana am mangay namen do ilaod, ori to namen ngiyani do rako a mahataw.
ch: 小時候我們家住在野銀部落，上學以後，就搬到了台灣本島，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Jivalino. After I started school, we moved to Taiwan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: mivatvatek ko rana do kakarangan a gako am, vilangan ko o monganay do kasasaodan no vazavazay no Yancoming a vakong, ori nipakatenngan ko rana do meyciamadadayan a iweywawalam no Yancoming do Taiwan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: ori, no ka teyteyka ya do nipiwalam namen am, yaken a ka ni kaka mehakay am nimakarala namen a omatao so apapnan namen a rararakeh, naknakmen namen kapakamizing so iweywawalam namen a Tao nokakoa.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到蘭嶼拜訪部落年老的親戚，想要聽聽自己族群從前的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: ya sikawao so kakawakawan rana o rarakeh, am no ya citaen am akman so malavayo pa a tao.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: apiaen na yaken ni akay a mapacici sia am, ipanci na o mabnek na ciriciring no peyciamahatawan. teymasonong o ipanci na ciriciring no tao, mangay do ciriciring no Ipong a ka no Amis a ka no ciriciring no dehdeh o katenngan na a ipanci.
ch: 徵得阿公同意訪問，他回答會說的語言別。他的達悟語說得很流利，連日本話和簡單的阿美語及漢語也會說。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Tao and he can even speak Japanese, simple Pangcah and Chinese.

ab: nipacici ko o rarakeh ori ipakateneng ko jia no kalialikey na pa am ninanao da no ciriciring no Ipong. no kalavayo na am nimasaneb a mangay do Taitong a manlangi do mogis no Amis, ori nipaciboang na. o ciriciring rana no dehdeh am, na nimahap do aro a kagagan na dehdeh, ori na ikaro no na katenengan a ipanci a ciriciring ang.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候上過日本教育；他年輕時，常去台東幫阿美族割稻打工，順便學阿美語；現在會說漢語是因為他有很多漢族朋友，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child.When he was young, he often went to Taitung to help the Amis harvest rice and work, and learned the Amis language; and he has many Han friends, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: no kapacici namen jira am, aro o malavat da monganay do makeyneynaapan a tao nokakoa a nitaid da a adan a teneteneng da.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於漁獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: tana namen ji a mimin a masilo no ciciloan a amizingen o da ipanci am, ciaha ta namen nipatoong do patowtowngan so ciriciring rana, apia no amizingen am to iwanga.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂他們說的話，還好我們有用錄音機錄音，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: no kalialikey ko pa am ji ko pa atenngi o meyciamadadayan a masaod no iweywawala namen, ori o ikasnek ko a ipanci da a Yancoming.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: ya to miparo o ya ko nimamizing am, ya ko rana katenngan o monganay do iweywawalam namen do pongso, ya mapeyzokab a ya ko pacikazwayan.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為達悟族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Tao is unique and something to be proud of.
